title: Betrayal at House on the Hill (3rd Edition)

walkthrough.title: Choose a Scenario Card
walkthrough.text: Let's get ready to explore a haunted house! In the third edition, the game starts by choosing one of five scenario cards, which determines which haunt will eventually trigger. Pick one at random and place it face up where everyone can see it.

walkthrough.title: Assemble the Starting Tiles
walkthrough.text: Let's build the ground floor of our haunted house by finding the three starting tiles: the Entrance Hall, the Foyer, and the Grand Staircase. These are the only tiles that begin on the board. Connect them in a line so every explorer starts in the same place.

walkthrough.title: Sort the Room Tiles by Floor
walkthrough.text: Let's organize the room tiles by separating them into three stacks based on the floor markings on the back: Ground Floor, Upper Floor, and Basement. Shuffle each stack face down. These will form the rooms you discover as you explore the house.

walkthrough.title: Shuffle the Card Decks
walkthrough.text: Let's find the three card decks: Event cards, Item cards, and Omen cards, each with a distinct card back. Shuffle each deck separately and place them face down within reach of all players.

walkthrough.title: Choose Your Explorer
walkthrough.text: Let's pick our characters! Each explorer tile is double-sided with two different characters sharing the same miniature, so pick whichever character appeals to you. Place the matching miniature in the Entrance Hall where all explorers begin.

walkthrough.title: Set Your Trait Sliders
walkthrough.text: Each explorer has four traits: Might, Speed, Knowledge, and Sanity, with numbers printed on the explorer tile. Find the green-highlighted starting values and set each plastic clip to that number. These sliders will go up and down throughout the game as events and items affect your explorer.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Dice
walkthrough.text: Let's find the special eight-sided dice included in the box, which have faces showing zero, one, or two pips. Place them in a central pool where everyone can reach them.

walkthrough.title: Set Out the Tokens
walkthrough.text: Let's organize the monster tokens, item pile tokens, and various effect markers by sorting them into groups near the board. You will not need most of them until the haunt begins, but having them ready saves time later.

walkthrough.title: Locate the Haunt Books
walkthrough.text: The box contains two critical booklets: the Traitor's Tome and the Secrets of Survival. Do not read either one yet. Set them aside face down where nobody can peek, because once the haunt triggers one side reads the Traitor's Tome and the other reads the Secrets of Survival.

walkthrough.title: Determine the First Player
walkthrough.text: The player whose explorer's birthday is closest to today goes first, or if there is a tie, pick the player with the highest starting Speed. Play proceeds clockwise from the first player.

Play proceeds clockwise

walkthrough.title: The Two Phases of the Game
walkthrough.text: Let's understand how Betrayal works at a high level. Phase one is cooperative exploration where everyone works together to discover rooms and gather items. Phase two is the haunt, where one player typically becomes the traitor and everyone else becomes the hero team with opposing objectives.

walkthrough.title: Your Turn: Move
walkthrough.text: On your turn, let's start by moving your explorer up to a number of spaces equal to your current Speed trait. Each room tile counts as one space, and you can move through any doorway that connects to another room you have already discovered.

walkthrough.title: Your Turn: Explore
walkthrough.text: Let's discover new rooms! When you move through a doorway that does not connect to an existing room, draw a room tile from the appropriate floor stack and place it so a doorway connects to where you entered. In the third edition, discovering a new room generally ends your movement for the turn.

walkthrough.title: Room Symbols and Card Draws
walkthrough.text: Many room tiles show a symbol in the corner: a spiral for Event, a bull's head for Item, or a raven for Omen. When you discover a room with one of these symbols, draw the matching card and resolve it immediately. Some rooms also have special effects printed directly on the tile.

walkthrough.title: Event Cards
walkthrough.text: Event cards describe something strange happening in the house and often require a trait roll where you roll dice equal to the relevant trait and add up the pips. Some events are beneficial and boost your traits, while others are harmful and lower them. Resolve the event and discard the card.

walkthrough.title: Item Cards
walkthrough.text: Item cards give your explorer equipment you keep for the rest of the game, such as weapons that boost Might or medical kits that heal traits. You can carry any number of items. Items stay with you until you trade them, drop them, or a game effect takes them away.

walkthrough.title: Omen Cards
walkthrough.text: Omen cards are special items or companions you keep, but each one brings the haunt closer. Let's think of them as ticking time bombs, because after resolving an Omen card you must immediately make a haunt roll. Examples include the Spear, the Dog, the Crystal Ball, and the Madman.

walkthrough.title: The Haunt Roll
walkthrough.text: After picking up an Omen, roll dice equal to the total number of Omens all players have discovered so far. If the total is five or more the haunt begins, and if it is less than five the game continues normally. The more Omens discovered, the more dice you roll and the more likely the haunt becomes.

walkthrough.title: Automatic Haunt Trigger
walkthrough.text: If the very last Omen card is drawn from the deck, the haunt triggers automatically regardless of the roll. This means the haunt will always happen eventually. Let's use the exploration phase wisely to prepare with items and strong traits.

walkthrough.title: The Four Traits
walkthrough.text: Let's look at your four traits: Might determines physical strength and combat power, Speed determines how many rooms you move per turn, Knowledge helps with puzzles and mental challenges, and Sanity protects from fear and madness effects. If any trait drops to the skull symbol, your explorer dies.

walkthrough.title: When the Haunt Begins
walkthrough.text: When the haunt is triggered, the scenario card tells you which haunt number to play and who becomes the traitor. The traitor takes the Traitor's Tome and leaves the room to read their haunt entry privately, while the remaining heroes read the Secrets of Survival together. Each side has different objectives they must keep secret from the other.

walkthrough.title: Player-Count Scaling
walkthrough.text: The third edition introduces player-count scaling for haunts, with sections labeled A, B, C, and D that adjust the difficulty based on how many players are at the table. Let's check the appropriate letter for our group size. This keeps haunts balanced whether you are playing with three explorers or six.

walkthrough.title: Combat
walkthrough.text: Let's learn how to fight: when an explorer attacks a monster or another explorer, both sides roll dice equal to their relevant trait, usually Might. Compare totals, and the difference between the two is the amount of damage dealt to the loser. Damage reduces the loser's traits.

walkthrough.title: Trait Damage and Death
walkthrough.text: When you take damage, you choose which trait to reduce: physical damage reduces Might or Speed, and mental damage reduces Knowledge or Sanity. If any single trait reaches the skull symbol at the bottom of its track, your explorer is killed. Other players can loot items from your corpse by entering your room.

walkthrough.title: No Default Stealing in Third Edition
walkthrough.text: Let's clarify an important third edition change: there is no default stealing rule, unlike previous editions where explorers could steal items with a trait roll. You can only take items from other players if a specific haunt rule or card effect allows it. You can still voluntarily trade items with willing players in the same room.

walkthrough.title: Winning the Game
walkthrough.text: The game ends when one side completes their haunt objective: heroes win by fulfilling the Secrets of Survival conditions, and the traitor wins by fulfilling the Traitor's Tome conditions. There are no victory points. It is a pure pass-or-fail objective for each team.

summary.title: The Two Phases of the Game
bullets: Phase 1: cooperative exploration — discover rooms, gather items/omens
Phase 2: haunt — traitor revealed, teams split, opposing objectives

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Haunted House
walkthrough.text: Welcome to your first game of Betrayal at House on the Hill! In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a five-player game focusing on the exploration phase before the haunt triggers. We will follow five explorers as they make some common beginner mistakes along the way.

walkthrough.title: Meet the Explorers
walkthrough.text: Let's meet our five explorers: Player one has Darrin with strong Might, Player two plays Zoe with high Knowledge, Player three picked Peter with balanced stats, Player four has Vivian with excellent Sanity, and Player five chose Brandon with the fastest starting Speed. All five begin together in the Entrance Hall.

walkthrough.title: Turn One: Darrin Explores
walkthrough.text: Player one moves Darrin through the Foyer doorway and draws a Ground Floor tile, the Organ Room with an Event symbol. Darrin draws an Event card that boosts his Might by one, and he slides his Might clip up one notch. Discovering that room ends his turn.

walkthrough.title: Turn One: Zoe Finds an Item
walkthrough.text: Player two moves Zoe through a different doorway and discovers the Dusty Hallway, which has an Item symbol. She draws the Medical Kit, which lets her heal one trait by two points as an action. Discovering the room ends her turn.

walkthrough.title: Turn One: Peter Finds the First Omen
walkthrough.text: Player three moves Peter upstairs via the Grand Staircase and discovers the Bloody Room on the Upper Floor, which shows an Omen symbol. Peter draws the Spear and must make a haunt roll: with one Omen in play he rolls one die and gets zero. The haunt does not trigger and play continues.

walkthrough.title: The Mistake: Hoarding Everything on One Explorer
walkthrough.text: Over the next few turns, Player three keeps pushing Peter into every Omen and Item room he can find, picking up the Dog, the Crystal Ball, and a second weapon. Player three figures that loading up one explorer makes that character unstoppable. By round three, Peter has three Omens and two Items while everyone else has almost nothing.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Spread Items and Omens Across the Group
walkthrough.text: Here is the problem: when the haunt triggers, the traitor is often the player who drew the most recent Omen, and if Peter becomes the traitor he takes all those Items and Omens with him. Many haunts strip the traitor's gear or turn it against the heroes. Let's spread discoveries across multiple explorers so that no single betrayal cripples the whole team.

walkthrough.title: Turn Four: Vivian Explores Cautiously
walkthrough.text: Player four takes a smarter approach with Vivian, moving through two already-discovered rooms before discovering one new room, the Chapel. The Chapel has an Event symbol that asks for a Sanity roll, and Vivian's high Sanity makes this easy, earning her a Knowledge boost.

walkthrough.title: Turn Four: Brandon Pushes Too Far
walkthrough.text: Player five decides to rush Brandon deep into unexplored territory on the Upper Floor, discovering a new room with an Event that lowers his Might by two. His Might was already mediocre, and now it is dangerously close to the skull. Brandon is isolated on the far end of the Upper Floor with no allies nearby.

walkthrough.title: The Mistake: Exploring Aggressively with Low Stats
walkthrough.text: Brandon made two errors at once: he explored aggressively without caring about his low Might, and he moved far away from the rest of the group. Events and rooms can punish weak traits severely. If the haunt triggers now, Brandon is alone with crippled stats and no items to defend himself.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Keep Stats Healthy and Stay Within Reach
walkthrough.text: Let's learn from Brandon's mistake: before pushing into unknown rooms, check your trait values, and if any trait is near the skull, play conservatively and focus on healing. Also keep at least one ally within a turn or two of movement. Once the haunt begins, isolation can be fatal because the traitor's monsters pick off loners easily.

walkthrough.title: Turn Five: Another Omen Appears
walkthrough.text: Player one has Darrin discover a new room with an Omen symbol and draws the Madman, bringing the total to four Omens in play across the group. Darrin rolls four dice for the haunt roll and gets a total of three, so the haunt still does not trigger. The odds are climbing fast though.

walkthrough.title: Turn Six: Zoe Discovers a Room and Forgets the Rule
walkthrough.text: Player two moves Zoe through an existing room, then discovers the Catacombs in the Basement and draws the Armor. Now Player two tries to keep moving Zoe to another doorway to discover a second room this turn, but the table stops her.

walkthrough.title: The Mistake: Ignoring the Room Discovery Rule
walkthrough.text: Player two forgot that in the third edition, discovering a new room generally ends your turn. You cannot keep moving after you place a new tile and resolve its effect. In older editions this was more lenient, but the third edition tightened this rule.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Discovering a Room Ends Your Turn
walkthrough.text: Let's remember this critical third edition rule: when you move through a doorway and draw a new room tile, your movement stops after resolving that room. Plan your turn carefully by reaching any desired existing rooms first before exploring into the unknown. This makes every exploration decision more consequential.

walkthrough.title: Turn Seven: The Haunt Triggers
walkthrough.text: Peter discovers another Omen room and draws the Ring, making five total Omens in play across all explorers. He rolls five dice and gets a total of six, triggering the haunt. The scenario card reveals which haunt to play and who becomes the traitor.

walkthrough.title: The Haunt Reveal
walkthrough.text: The scenario card identifies Peter as the traitor, and remember how he hoarded three Omens and multiple Items. Peter takes the Traitor's Tome and leaves the room while the other four heroes read the Secrets of Survival together. The exploration phase is over and the real horror begins.

walkthrough.title: Key Takeaways
walkthrough.text: Let's recap the three big lessons from our practice game. First, spread items and omens across multiple explorers so the traitor does not walk away with everything. Second, do not explore aggressively with low stats or while isolated, and third, remember that discovering a new room ends your turn in the third edition.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations and Next Steps
walkthrough.text: Congratulations on surviving your first exploration phase, where you learned the core flow of move, explore, draw cards, and roll for the haunt! For deeper strategies check out the General Tips and Advanced Strategies sections. You can also ask your personal GM Guide anything during your game by tapping the Q and A tab.

walkthrough.title: Explore with Purpose
walkthrough.text: Let's not wander aimlessly through the house, because every room you discover could trigger an Event, grant an Item, or introduce an Omen that pushes the haunt closer. Plan which floor you want to explore based on what your explorer needs. If your Might is low, look for rooms that might yield weapons or stat boosts rather than pushing into dangerous territory.

walkthrough.title: Balance Omen Collection Across the Group
walkthrough.text: Let's talk about who should be picking up Omens: since the haunt roll happens after every Omen draw, and the traitor is often connected to whoever triggered the haunt, you want Omens spread across multiple explorers. If one player has collected three Omens and becomes the traitor, the hero team loses access to all of those Omen items at once.

walkthrough.title: Stay Close Before the Haunt
walkthrough.text: Spreading out too thin during the exploration phase is one of the most punishing mistakes in Betrayal. When the haunt starts, heroes often need to regroup, share items, or coordinate against the traitor's forces. Let's keep explorers within a few rooms of each other so nobody wastes three or four turns just walking back.

walkthrough.title: Manage Your Trait Sliders Carefully
walkthrough.text: Let's treat our four traits like a resource to be managed: if Might is low avoid combat rooms, if Sanity is near the skull stay away from horror-themed Events. Use healing items before your traits reach critical levels. A dead explorer cannot help anyone.

walkthrough.title: Read the Haunt Rules Carefully
walkthrough.text: When the haunt triggers, both sides need to read their instructions thoroughly before starting play. Heroes should discuss their strategy openly once the traitor leaves the room. The traitor should take notes on monster placement, special rules, and victory conditions.

walkthrough.title: Heroes Must Communicate and Cooperate
walkthrough.text: Once the haunt begins, let's work as a team by sharing information about what we read in the Secrets of Survival and coordinating who handles which objective. If one hero has the weapon and another has the ritual item, plan how to get them to the right room. Solo heroics rarely work because the traitor usually has action economy advantages through monsters.

walkthrough.title: The Traitor Should Act Decisively
walkthrough.text: If you become the traitor, do not play passively because you typically have monsters, special powers, or environmental effects at your disposal. Read your haunt entry carefully to understand your win condition and execute a plan immediately. The longer you wait, the more time the heroes have to organize.

walkthrough.title: Use the Player-Count Scaling
walkthrough.text: Let's not forget the third edition's A, B, C, and D scaling sections in each haunt, which exist to keep the game fair at every player count. Make sure you are reading the correct scaling letter for your group size. Playing a six-player scaling in a three-player game can make the haunt feel impossibly hard or trivially easy.

summary.title: Explore with Purpose
bullets: Plan which floor to explore based on your explorer's needs
Low Might → seek weapons/stat boosts instead of dangerous rooms
Every room draw carries risk and reward

summary.title: Balance Omen Collection Across the Group
bullets: Spread Omens across multiple explorers
Traitor often = whoever triggered the haunt
If 1 player has all Omens and turns traitor → heroes lose everything

summary.title: Stay Close Before the Haunt
bullets: Heroes need to regroup when haunt starts
Isolated explorers waste turns walking back
Keep everyone within a few rooms of each other

summary.title: Manage Your Trait Sliders Carefully
bullets: Treat traits as resources to manage
Avoid challenges targeting your weakest trait
Heal before reaching critical levels — dead explorers help nobody

summary.title: Read the Haunt Rules Carefully
bullets: Both sides: read instructions thoroughly before resuming play
Heroes discuss strategy while traitor is away
Traitor: take notes on monsters, rules, win conditions

summary.title: Heroes Must Communicate and Cooperate
bullets: Share Secrets of Survival info openly among heroes
Coordinate objectives: who carries what, who goes where
Solo heroics fail — traitor has action economy via monsters

summary.title: The Traitor Should Act Decisively
bullets: Use monsters, powers, and environmental effects aggressively
Execute your plan immediately — delay favors the heroes
Read haunt entry carefully for win conditions

summary.title: Use the Player-Count Scaling
bullets: 3rd Ed haunts have A/B/C/D sections for group size
Always check the correct letter for your player count
Wrong scaling = unbalanced haunt

walkthrough.title: Map Architecture Matters
walkthrough.text: Advanced players think about the house layout as a strategic asset, because when you place a new room tile you often have a choice about which doorway to connect and how to orient it. Let's create efficient pathways between floors and avoid dead-end branches that trap explorers. A well-connected house helps heroes regroup quickly when the haunt triggers.

walkthrough.title: Controlling the Haunt Timing
walkthrough.text: Let's use the limited control we have over when the haunt triggers: if your team feels prepared with good items and strong traits, push for Omen rooms to trigger it on your terms. If you feel weak, let other explorers discover rooms while you backtrack to heal or trade. The haunt roll probability curve means you ideally want to trigger around the third or fourth Omen.

walkthrough.title: Pre-Haunt Item Positioning
walkthrough.text: Before the haunt triggers, let's think about which items pair well together and who should carry them: weapons belong on high-Might explorers, healing items on frontline fighters, and mobility items on explorers who may need to reach distant objectives. This pre-positioning saves critical turns once the haunt begins.

walkthrough.title: Reading the Traitor
walkthrough.text: Once the haunt begins, let's watch the traitor's behavior for clues about their objective. If the traitor is rushing toward a specific room, that room is probably important to their win condition. Let's use this information to cut them off or guard key locations.

walkthrough.title: Monster Management as the Traitor
walkthrough.text: Most haunts give the traitor control over monsters with separate turns, and advanced traitors use them strategically rather than just attacking the nearest hero. Let's block doorways to slow hero movement, send monsters to guard objective rooms, and use multiple monsters to overwhelm a single isolated hero. Action economy is the traitor's greatest weapon.

walkthrough.title: Hero Focus Fire
walkthrough.text: When heroes fight the traitor or their monsters, let's coordinate our attacks because two heroes attacking the same monster in one round is far more effective than splitting attacks. Focus combat on the biggest threat first, whether that is the traitor themselves or a powerful monster guarding your objective. Eliminate threats quickly so you can redirect to your win condition.

walkthrough.title: Trading and Corpse Looting
walkthrough.text: Let's not overlook the third edition's item economy during the haunt: heroes in the same room can trade items freely on their turn, and if an explorer dies other players can enter that room to loot items from the corpse. If a hero carrying a critical item is about to die, try to trade that item to a safer explorer before it is too late.

walkthrough.title: Adapting to Your Scenario
walkthrough.text: Each scenario in the third edition creates a different game experience, and after playing a few haunts you will start to recognize patterns in how they unfold. Some haunts reward aggressive exploration while others reward defensive positioning, and some make combat central while others focus on puzzles and rituals. Let's adapt our pre-haunt strategy based on which scenario card was chosen at setup.

summary.title: Map Architecture Matters
bullets: Orient room tiles to create efficient pathways between floors
Avoid dead-end branches that trap explorers
Well-connected house helps heroes regroup after haunt

summary.title: Controlling the Haunt Timing
bullets: Prepared (good items + stats) → push for Omen rooms
Weak → let others explore, backtrack to heal/trade
Optimal trigger: around 3rd or 4th Omen

summary.title: Pre-Haunt Item Positioning
bullets: Weapons → high-Might explorers
Healing → frontline fighters | Mobility → objective runners
Pre-positioning saves turns once haunt begins

summary.title: Reading the Traitor
bullets: Watch traitor's movement for clues about their objective
Rushing to a specific room = probably important to their win condition
Use behavioral info to cut them off

summary.title: Monster Management as the Traitor
bullets: Block doorways to slow hero movement
Guard objective rooms with monster placement
Overwhelm isolated heroes — do not spread attacks evenly

summary.title: Hero Focus Fire
bullets: Coordinate 2+ heroes on the same target
Eliminate biggest threat first (traitor or key monster)
Clear threats quickly → redirect to win condition

summary.title: Trading and Corpse Looting
bullets: Heroes in same room can trade items freely
Dead explorer's items can be looted from their room
Trade critical items to safer heroes before death

summary.title: Adapting to Your Scenario
bullets: Each scenario card creates a different game experience
Some reward aggression, others reward defensive positioning
Adapt pre-haunt strategy based on the chosen scenario

entries.definition: The first phase of the game where all players cooperate to explore the house, discover rooms, and collect cards. This phase ends when the haunt is triggered by a haunt roll of five or more, or when the last Omen card is drawn.

The second phase of the game that begins when the haunt is triggered. Players split into the traitor team and the hero team, each with secret objectives. The game ends when one side completes their haunt objective.
A physical trait representing raw strength. Used in combat rolls and physical challenges. If Might reaches the skull symbol, the explorer dies.
A physical trait representing agility and movement. Determines how many rooms an explorer can move through per turn. If Speed reaches the skull symbol, the explorer dies.
A mental trait representing intelligence and education. Used for puzzle-solving and resisting mental challenges. If Knowledge reaches the skull symbol, the explorer dies.
A mental trait representing willpower and mental fortitude. Used to resist fear, madness, and supernatural effects. If Sanity reaches the skull symbol, the explorer dies.

A plastic clip on the explorer tile that tracks the current value of each trait. Sliders move up when traits are boosted and down when traits take damage. The green-highlighted number is the starting value.
A card drawn when discovering a room with the spiral symbol. Events describe strange occurrences and often require trait rolls. They can boost or lower traits and are discarded after resolving.
A card drawn when discovering a room with the bull's head symbol. Items are equipment kept by the explorer and provide ongoing benefits such as weapons, healing tools, or special abilities.

A card drawn when discovering a room with the raven symbol. Omens are kept like items but each one triggers a mandatory haunt roll. Examples include the Spear, the Dog, the Crystal Ball, and the Madman.

The main level of the house containing the starting tiles. Room tiles with Ground Floor markings on the back are drawn when exploring doorways on this level. The Entrance Hall, Foyer, and Grand Staircase are the starting Ground Floor tiles.
The second level of the house accessed via the Grand Staircase or other stairway tiles. Room tiles with Upper Floor markings are drawn when exploring doorways on this level.

The underground level of the house accessed via specific stairway tiles. Room tiles with Basement markings are drawn when exploring doorways on this level. Basement rooms tend to have more dangerous events.
A cardboard tile representing a room in the house. Each tile shows doorways, room names, and may include card symbols or special effects. Tiles are drawn from floor-specific stacks when exploring.

A dice roll made immediately after drawing an Omen card. Roll dice equal to the total number of Omens discovered by all players. A result of five or more triggers the haunt. Fewer Omens means fewer dice, making early haunts rare.

The player who opposes the hero team after the haunt triggers. The traitor's identity is determined by the scenario card and the specific haunt conditions. The traitor reads the Traitor's Tome for their secret objectives.

A booklet containing the traitor's instructions for each haunt scenario. The traitor reads their entry privately and keeps their objectives hidden from the hero team. Includes monster stats, special rules, and victory conditions.

A booklet containing the hero team's instructions for each haunt scenario. All heroes read their entry together and plan strategy openly. Includes the heroes' victory conditions and any special rules for their side.
One of five cards chosen at the start of the game that determines which haunt will be triggered and how the traitor is identified. Each scenario card provides a different set of possible haunts.
A third edition feature where each haunt entry includes sections labeled A, B, C, and D that adjust difficulty based on the number of players. This keeps haunts balanced from three to six players.
A dice roll made during combat using dice equal to the relevant trait, usually Might. Both the attacker and defender roll, and the difference between totals determines damage dealt to the loser.
Damage that reduces physical traits: Might or Speed. The damaged player chooses how to distribute the damage across their physical traits. If either reaches the skull, the explorer dies.
Damage that reduces mental traits: Knowledge or Sanity. The damaged player chooses how to distribute the damage across their mental traits. If either reaches the skull, the explorer dies.

A playable character in the game represented by a miniature and a double-sided character tile. Each explorer has unique starting trait values and a backstory. Explorers move through the house, discover rooms, and participate in the haunt.

A third edition rule that allows players to pick up items from a dead explorer's room. When an explorer dies, their items remain in the room where they fell. Any explorer entering that room can take items from the corpse.

A creature controlled by the traitor during the haunt phase. Monsters have their own stats and movement rules defined in the Traitor's Tome. They act on the traitor's turn and can attack heroes or block movement.
The lowest position on a trait slider representing death. If any single trait is reduced to the skull symbol, the explorer is immediately killed regardless of their other trait values.
Special eight-sided dice with faces showing zero, one, or two pips. Multiple dice are rolled and pips are summed for trait checks, combat, and haunt rolls. The average result per die is one pip.
